content:
Member-only story

Angular

Java

Software Development

Programming

Technology

One fintech codebase revealed why frontend, backend, and distributed systems kept failing for the exact same reason.

5 min read
2 hours ago

--

For eight months, my team blamed three different things for the same outage.

• First Angular — too many active subscriptions, memory climbing under load, zone change detection firing where it shouldn’t.

• Then Java — synchronous downstream calls stacking up, GC pauses hitting 400ms under sustained traffic.

• Then the service topology itself — nine services where three would have done it.
We rewrote the Angular module. We refactored the Java service. We collapsed two microservices. The outages kept happening. Same symptom pattern, different layer called, every sprint.
What we weren’t looking at was the single Postgres connection pool shared across four of those nine services, sized for staging load, never touched during any of the three rewrites.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Angular didn’t cause the outage. Java didn’t cause it. Neither did the microservice split.
The monitoring gap that let a connection pool run hot for eight months — that’s what caused it. The technologies were just where we…
